# ArchGen-AI-generated-cores

[](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

**This repository is dedicated to open-sourcing processor core RTL generated by AI methods, such as Large Language Models (LLMs).**

## 🚀 About This Project

Large Language Models (LLMs) are beginning to show unique potential in the field of hardware design. This project aims to explore the feasibility of using AI to automatically generate, iterate, and optimize RISC-V processor cores.

By open-sourcing all AI-generated RTL, we provide a public repository for the community to study, test, verify, and improve AI-generated hardware. We hope to observe the capabilities and limitations of AI in understanding complex computer architectures, including concepts like pipelining, data hazards, and bypassing.

## 🗺️ Planned Core Versions

We are progressively increasing the complexity of the AI-generated cores. Below is our current development roadmap and status:

  * ✅ **Single-Cycle RV32I** (Completed)
  * ✅ **5-Stage Pipeline RV32I (Stall-based)** (Completed)
  * ⏳ **5-Stage Pipeline RV32I (Bypass-based)** (In Progress)
  * ▶️ **5-Stage Pipeline RV32I with 'M' Extension (Bypass-based)** (Upcoming)

-----

## ⚠️ A Note on AI-Generated Code

Using AI (especially LLMs) to generate RTL is an experimental process. Users should be aware of the following when using the code in this repository:

  * **Functional Correctness:** AI-generated code may contain logical bugs, unhandled edge cases, or even "hallucinations" that deviate from the architecture specification. **All code should be rigorously simulated and verified before use.**
  * **Code Style:** The style and readability of the code may differ from human-engineered RTL.
  * **Synthesis Performance:** We make no guarantees regarding the timing, area, or power (PPA) of the AI-generated code.

The primary goal of this project is **to explore feasibility**, not to produce production-ready (tapeout-ready) designs.

## 📂 Repository Structure (Suggestion)

To keep the project organized, we suggest the following structure (you can modify this as needed):

```
.
├── /archgen_single_rv32i           # ✅ Single-Cycle Core
│   ├── /rtl                      # Verilog / VHDL code
│   ├── /sim                      # Simulation scripts and Testbenches
│   └── /doc                      # AI generation prompts or related notes
│
├── /archgen_pipeline_rv32i         # ✅ Stall-based Pipeline
│   ├── /rtl
│   ├── /sim
│   └── /doc
│
├── /archgen_pipeline_bypass_rv32i        # ⏳ Bypass-based Pipeline
│   ├── ...
│
└── README.md                     # This file
```

## 🤝 How to Contribute

We strongly welcome contributions from the community\! Whether you are a hardware engineer, an AI researcher, or a RISC-V enthusiast, you can help in several ways:

1.  **Test and Verify:** Fork this repository, write testbenches for the existing cores, and [submit Issues](https://www.google.com/search?q=https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/ArchGen-AI-generated-cores/issues) for any bugs you find.
2.  **Submit Pull Requests:** If you fix a bug or have optimized the AI generation prompts, feel free to submit a PR.
3.  **Share Your Generations:** If you have used AI to generate a core that is on our roadmap but not yet started (like the 'M' extension), we encourage you to contribute it\!
